Rohit Kodical, Esq. of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (415-775-1206), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Beatrice Kiras, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT HILL BROTHERS CHEMICAL COMPANY'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER PRECLUDING THE DEPOSITION OF HILL BROTHERS' CUSTODIAN OF RECORDS AND PERSON MOST QUALIFIED

Hearing held. The Court ordered continued defendant Hill Brothers Chemical Company's motion to January 6, 2012 at 10:00 a.m. in Department 503 for an informal meeting. Tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion for protective order precluding the deposition of its custodian of records/person most qualified is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate good cause to preclude the deposition in its entirety, as required by C.C.P. 2025.420. Defendant did not demonstrate that the deposition of its representative in this case would be duplicative of any prior testimony and document production. In order for defendant to prepare their person most knowledgeable, though, it is ordered that plaintiffs must identify the jobsites in Sacramento while employed by Ted J. Cook Painting Co. between 1965-1975 and while employed by the County of Sacramento 1975-1995 and what products were used. Defendant's and plaintiffs' requests for sanctions are denied. The Court reserves its ruling on the scope of deposition. Parties are to prepare and bring proposed orders to the meeting. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Brandon Combs, CSR # 12978 = (503/TLJ)
